title: Container queries

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Container queries; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Snapshot 2024; W3C Working Group Note 2024; provisions applicable to Container queries
sources.claim: Container queries style a component from an ancestor query container's available size or style, enabling context-independent composition.

sources.locator: MDN CSS Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Container queries
sources.claim: Container queries style a component from an ancestor query container's available size or style, enabling context-independent composition.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Container queries style a component from an ancestor query container's available size or style, enabling context-independent composition.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: The same card switches to a side-by-side layout only when its panel container is wide enough. Your goal is to build and explain a component with a size-based container query and safe baseline. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Flexbox and Grid; Typography, color and visual treatment; Media and feature queries; Stylesheet organization. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Container queries style a component from an ancestor query container's available size or style, enabling context-independent composition. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: A query container must be established, containment affects sizing, and viewport concerns still need media queries. This draft therefore rejects: Container queries measure the component's own final size without any configured ancestor.

includes: Adapt reusable components to their available container rather than only the viewport.
Build and explain a component with a size-based container query and safe baseline.
excludes: A query container must be established, containment affects sizing, and viewport concerns still need media queries.

outcomes: Build and explain a component with a size-based container query and safe baseline.

examples.title: Container queries: concrete case
examples.situation: The same card switches to a side-by-side layout only when its panel container is wide enough.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: The same card switches to a side-by-side layout only when its panel container is wide enough.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Define the intended query container and style from that container's conditions, not an assumed page viewport.
examples.result: The result follows because Define the intended query container and style from that container's conditions, not an assumed page viewport.

examples.title: Container queries: boundary case
examples.situation: A query container must be established, containment affects sizing, and viewport concerns still need media queries.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: A query container must be established, containment affects sizing, and viewport concerns still need media queries.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Container queries measure the component's own final size without any configured ancestor.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Define the intended query container and style from that container's conditions, not an assumed page viewport.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Use a concrete CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling artifact to explain why this belief fails: Container queries measure the component's own final size without any configured ancestor.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Container queries, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Container queries before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
